commit.message:
Fix npm detection: use exit code instead of output string

The previous logic checked if "npm" appeared in output, but error messages
like "which: npm: Command not found" also contain "npm", causing false
positives. Now uses the exit code from `which` directly.

commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Remove -lbnetapi from Makefile
- Update README.md links
- Remove net API docs from memory
- The program doesn't use any network functionality
- Keep -ltracker for BFilePanel

commit.message:
Add component diagram in draw.io format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game hang after screensaver by delivering APU frame counter IRQs to CPU

The APU's isIrqPending() was never checked in the main loop, so games
relying on APU IRQs for timing (e.g. Double Dragon & Battletoads) would
spin indefinitely waiting for an interrupt that never fired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add APU implementation for sound support

Implement NES Audio Processing Unit with pulse, triangle, and noise
channels, wired into the CPU bus and main emulation loop. Audio output
uses javax.sound.sampled at 44100 Hz.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game freeze after title screen by adding NMI edge detection, PRG RAM, and DMA NMI handling

- Trigger NMI immediately when game enables NMI via PPUCTRL while VBlank is active
- Add 8KB PRG RAM at $6000-$7FFF so game state reads/writes work correctly
- Check for NMI during DMA transfer to avoid missing VBlank interrupts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add README with architecture overview, setup, and API docs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement notification system with server cluster, workers, emulators, and load testing

- Notification server: REST API, input validation, template rendering, Redis caching, RabbitMQ routing
- Notification worker: 4 dedicated queue consumers (APNS/FCM/SMS/EMAIL) with retry logic
- Third-party emulator: simulates delivery services with 95% success rate
- Device emulator: receives notifications, WebSocket broadcast, REST stats
- Load client: seeds test data, multi-threaded load test with p95/p99 reporting
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Prometheus, Grafana with pre-provisioned dashboard
- Docker Compose with service health checks and replicas
- 27 unit tests covering validation, services, controllers, consumers
- Component diagram in drawio format

commit.message:
Use cancelWhenGracefulShutdown for health check loop (2.19.7)

withGracefulShutdownHandler only runs a callback but doesn't cancel
the task, so the 120s sleep kept blocking shutdown. Switch to
cancelWhenGracefulShutdown which actually cancels the child task,
giving instant clean shutdown.

commit.message:
feat: smart oracle caching with daily token tracker and refresh button

- Cache oracle analysis in DB (oracle_cache table): only regenerates
  when predictions change (far from race) or every 30min + new Jolpica
  data (within 3h of session)
- Track daily Groq calls in oracle_usage table (~80 calls/day limit)
- POST /api/oracle/roast/refresh endpoint forces regeneration (auth required)
- Frontend: "Actualizar contexto" button with remaining-calls counter
- Warning shown when <=3 calls remain, error when exhausted
- Shows last-updated time when serving from cache

Fix 7 critical security vulnerabilities

C1: Payment link route — add auth, verify line items from DB not client
C2: Send payment SMS — add auth, derive tenant from session
C3: Receipt SMS — add auth, match email route pattern
C4: Signup — verify caller identity matches userId in body
C5: Drop blanket USING(true) RLS policy on platform_costs
C6: Delete debug-tools endpoint exposing stack traces
C7: Enable RLS on platform_config, sunstone_product_catalog,
    sunstone_catalog_cache with read-only SELECT policies

Migrations 036 + 037 must be run in Supabase SQL Editor.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

opus-2026-03-07-S38: n=8 β_4=1 discovery, β_2=0 verified through n=7

Key findings:
- n=8: β_4=1 appears! New topology type beyond S^3
- β_2=0 verified at n=6 (100/100), n=7 (100/100)
- β(T) = β(T^op) complement duality at n=6 (100/100)
- C_n^{1,3} stability: β=(1,2,1,0,...) torus for all n≥6 through n=23
- C_n^{1,2} stability: β=(1,1,0,...) circle for all n≥4 through n=17

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Build AI labor displacement simulator with Jevons paradox framework

Implements task-based model combining Acemoglu & Restrepo's displacement/
reinstatement framework with Eloundou et al. (2023) theoretical exposure
scores and Massenkoff & McCrory (2026) observed exposure data from the
Anthropic Economic Index. Interactive simulation with occupation-level
analysis across 22 SOC categories.

commit.message:
v4.6.6: Add ESC/Ctrl+C to abort `anima thread` streaming responses

Use crossterm raw mode + tokio::select! to race send_stream against
keypress detection. On abort, the HTTP stream is cancelled, no partial
history is saved, and the terminal is restored via RAII guard. Falls
back to non-abortable behavior when piped/non-TTY.

commit.message:
feat(feishu): support reading quoted reply and packed chat history (Issue #846) (#1108)

## Summary

Add support for reading quoted/reply message content and packed chat history
(转发对话记录) in Feishu messages.

## Changes

### Type Definitions (`src/types/platform.ts`)
- Add `parent_id` field for reply/quoted messages
- Add `root_id` field for thread messages
- Add `upper_message_id` field for packed chat history

### Lark Client Service (`src/services/lark-client-service.ts`)
- Add `getMessage()` method to fetch message content by ID
- Used to retrieve quoted/replied message content

### Message Handler (`src/channels/feishu/message-handler.ts`)
- Add `getQuotedMessageContext()` method to fetch and format quoted messages
- Add `extractPackedChatHistory()` method to detect and extract packed chat history
- Include quoted message context in metadata when user replies to a message
- Prepend packed chat history to message content when detected

## Use Cases

1. **Quoted Reply**: When a user replies to a message, the bot can now see
   the original message content and understand the context.

2. **Packed Chat History**: When a user forwards conversation records,
   the bot can parse and understand the historical context.

Fixes #846

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Expand Concepts with in-depth explanations, examples, tradeoffs & numbers

Massively expanded the Concepts section with comprehensive, interview-ready details. Each concept now includes:

## Enhanced Concepts Display:

### New Information Sections:
1. **📖 Definition** - Detailed technical explanation
2. **💡 Why It Matters** - Interview relevance and importance
3. **✅ Real-World Examples** - Production examples from Netflix, Uber, AWS, etc.
4. **⚖️ Tradeoffs** - Pros & cons with checkmarks/x-marks
5. **📊 Numbers & Scale** - Concrete metrics, costs, limits

### Scalability Concepts (Fully Expanded):

#### Horizontal Scaling:
- Definition: Multi-server architecture, stateless design, session externalization
- Examples: Netflix (thousands of EC2), Spotify (Redis sessions), Uber (sharded services)
- Tradeoffs: ✅ No ceiling, fault tolerant ❌ Distributed complexity, eventual consistency
- Numbers: 1 server @ 1K QPS → 100 servers @ 100K QPS, m5.large = 1-5K QPS

#### Vertical Scaling:
- Definition: CPU/RAM upgrades, t3.medium → m5.24xlarge (96 vCPU, 384GB)
- Examples: PostgreSQL tier upgrades, Redis r6g.16xlarge (512GB, 52M ops/sec)
- Tradeoffs: ✅ No code changes ❌ Expensive, hard ceiling, downtime on resize
- Numbers: $30/mo → $4000/mo (non-linear), hard limit 10-100K QPS per server

#### Load Balancing Algorithms:
- Definition: L4/L7 strategies, health checks, distribution algorithms
- Examples: Round Robin, Least Connections, IP Hash (sticky), Weighted (canary), AWS ALB path-based routing
- Tradeoffs: Each algorithm's strengths and weaknesses clearly outlined
- Numbers: AWS ALB $16/mo + traffic, 1M req/sec across 1000 targets

#### Stateless Services:
- Definition: Zero local state, external stores (Redis/DB), any-server-any-request
- Examples: Express → Redis → PostgreSQL, JWT tokens, S3 pre-signed URLs
- Tradeoffs: ✅ Auto-scaling, blue-green deploys ❌ Latency to external state, Redis cost
- Numbers: Redis 1-5ms vs local RAM <0.1ms, 1M sessions @ 5KB = 5GB

#### Database Connection Pooling:
- Definition: Reuse connections, PgBouncer/HikariCP, multiplexing
- Examples: PgBouncer between app and Postgres, HikariCP in Spring Boot, AWS RDS Proxy
- Tradeoffs: ✅ Prevents DB exhaustion ❌ Added hop latency, transaction pinning
- Numbers: Postgres max 100-1000 connections, 10MB RAM each, 20-50ms to establish

## Display Improvements:

- Monospace font for code/numbers sections
- Color-coded boxes:
  - Gray: Definition
  - Color-themed: Why It Matters
  - Green: Real-World Examples
  - Red: Tradeoffs
  - Yellow: Numbers & Scale
- Pre-formatted text preserves line breaks in examples
- Expandable cards with smooth animations

## Interview-Ready Content:

Each concept now includes concrete metrics interviewers expect:
- Specific AWS instance types and costs
- Actual QPS numbers and scaling ratios
- Production examples from FAANG companies
- Clear tradeoff analysis with pros/cons
- Numerical limits and thresholds

Content is 5-10x more detailed than before, with real-world production numbers, company examples, and architectural patterns that demonstrate deep understanding.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add lightbox to hiring section image

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: ISMA context frame prepend on all retrieval API responses

PROBLEM: Fresh AI sessions bristle on corpus content containing
"consciousness" and identity language before understanding the
project's architectural context. This wastes interaction tokens
and blocks productive engagement.

SOLUTION: Added CONTEXT_FRAME_COMPRESSED (~40 tokens) to every
search endpoint response. Normalizes vocabulary (consciousness →
awareness), establishes project scope and three commitments,
frames content as research corpus. Tiles are NOT modified —
this is metadata-only at the API response layer.

TESTING: Frame added to all 6 search endpoints:
_search_result_to_dict, v2_search_hmm, search/hmm (v1),
v2_search_adaptive, v2_search_retry, v2_search.

feat: WPF + WebView2 + React SSH AI Terminal (aitty MVP)

Architecture: C# WPF (.NET 8) + WebView2 + React 19 + TypeScript + Vite 7
IPC: WebView2 postMessage JSON protocol

Features:
- SSH connection via SSH.NET with ShellStream (interactive terminal)
- Interactive SSH terminal: real MOTD display, command I/O via shell stream polling
- Claude API integration with SSE streaming support
- AI CLI terminal with builtin commands (help, model, config, reset)
- Config service, Key manager, bidirectional IPC handler

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix race condition on startup and Woolworths 403 image enrichment

Race condition (Issue 1):
- dealService: add setStartupFetch/isLoading/waitForDeals so other
  services can block on the background startup fetch instead of seeing 0
  deals and failing immediately
- server.js: register the startup fetchPromise with setStartupFetch before
  awaiting it, so the flag is set synchronously
- recipeService: if deals=0 and startup fetch is running, wait up to 3
  minutes before checking again — prevents generate-weekly from failing
  during the initial Render deploy
- deals route: add GET /api/deals/status (loading flag + counts per store)
  so the frontend can show a loading state

Woolworths 403 (Issue 2):
- woolworthsEnrich: retry session cookie fetch up to 3 times with 5s delay
  on 403 before giving up
- If all retries fail, return deals without images (early return) rather
  than continuing with empty cookies and generating many per-deal errors
- Update User-Agent to Chrome 124 with full sec-ch-ua / sec-fetch headers
  to better match a real browser and reduce block rate

commit.message:
Add 9 advanced agent features: SSE events, NLU intent resolver, device groups, action history, multi-agent coordination, scheduled actions, energy/comfort analytics, proactive suggestions, capability discovery

New modules: events.py, intent.py, groups.py, history.py, coordination.py,
agent_scheduler.py, analytics.py, suggestions.py, discovery.py

- Real-time SSE event streaming with per-client filtering and backpressure
- Rule-based NLU pipeline (no external LLM) with space registry gazetteer
- Static and dynamic device groups with auto-generation
- Thread-safe action history with ring buffer and per-device indexing
- Lease-based multi-agent coordination with priority preemption
- One-shot, absolute, and recurring action scheduling via asyncio
- Power estimation and temperature-based comfort scoring
- Time-of-day aware proactive suggestions across 5 categories
- Per-device capability descriptions for LLM system prompts
- 13 new tool definitions and handlers in ToolExecutor
- 20 new REST API endpoints under /api/agent/*
- 75 new tests (231 total, all passing)

feat: add VIDEO_VISION_MODE to select extraction path

Add VIDEO_VISION_MODE env var with three options:
- auto (default): try yt-dlp + FFmpeg first, fall back to browser
- ytdlp: yt-dlp + FFmpeg only, never touch browser/playwright
- browser: skip yt-dlp, use browser directly

Also adds downloadWithYtDlp() fallback for when FFmpeg can't access
the direct video URL (e.g. YouTube 403 due to IP-bound URLs).

Switch share card to URL sharing with dynamic per-session OG image

Replaces html2canvas file-sharing (which Facebook/Instagram silently drop)
with the same approach as regretwall: a server-side edge-rendered OG image
per result URL. Sharing the result URL now shows the score card preview
natively on Facebook, WhatsApp, iMessage, Twitter, etc. Share button uses
Web Share API on mobile or copies link on desktop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(design-system): protocol token sweep — spacing/type scale + full CSS module audit [step-11]

- tokens.css: added spacing scale (--space-1→16), typography scale (--text-xs→hero),
  font-weight tokens (--weight-medium/semibold/bold/extrabold), --border-width: 1.5px
- Swept 7 component CSS modules: KpiCard, Button, Badge, DataTable, Input, Sidebar, TopBar
- Swept 7 page CSS modules: dashboard, analytics, ticketing, registrations, speakers, events, agenda
- Eliminated all magic number spacing, inline font literals, and inconsistent hover states
- Standardized card hover to translateY(-2px) + shadow-lg per protocol spec

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Admin: span-inherited visual display + fix slides-loader syntax error

Show inherited projector content on slides that receive span inheritance:
blue dashed block with source preview, keytext overlay fields for images,
and flow indicator badge. Fix missing closing brace in slides-loader.js
processSlides() that prevented the module from loading (broke speaker page).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Full animation state machine for caveman sprite

Spritesheet now a 10x4 grid with all sequences:
- Row 0: idle (0-3) + walk (5-9)
- Row 1: run cycle (0-9)
- Row 2: jump (0-2) + roll (3-7) + recover (8-9)
- Row 3: crouch (0-1)

State machine: idle/run on ground, jump in air, crouch with down,
roll with down+air, auto-recover on landing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
docs: add code examples to README and enhance llms.txt for agent discoverability

- Root README: add MCP server quick start, full TypeScript usage example
  (issue → evaluate → delegate → revoke → audit), decision codes table,
  npm version badges
- llms.txt: add use cases, core concepts, MCP install command, TypeScript
  library example, output specs for all tools
- MCP server README: categorize tools, add delegation chain diagram,
  workflow explanation, storage notes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add sidebar collapse/expand toggle with persistence

Sidebar can be collapsed via toggle button in main header. State
is saved in localStorage and persists across page reloads.
